api_base.description: Base URL of the instance's API
api_base.title: Api Base

protocol_versions.description: Federation protocol versions this instance supports. Peers negotiate to a common version.
protocol_versions.title: Protocol Versions

public_key.description: Ed25519 public key, URL-safe base64 (no padding)
public_key.title: Public Key

civil_date.description: Local civil date. Defaults to today (UTC) — pass an explicit date for backfill or for non-UTC observers.
civil_date.title: Civil Date

observed_at.description: When the adoration was performed. Defaults to server time at receipt.
observed_at.title: Observed At

AggregateResult.description:
One noised aggregate. All four NoisyAggregate fields surface
so the reader can judge the noise (b108-2hr honesty rule).
AggregateResult.title: AggregateResult

AliasNeighbour.description: One entity reachable from the focus entity via the alias-graph.
AliasNeighbour.title: AliasNeighbour

AttestationCreate.description:
Body for ``POST /attestations``.

The caller supplies either:
* ``self_signature`` + ``signer_public_key`` (the signature is
  computed client-side; the server only verifies and persists), OR
* ``private_key`` bytes (the server signs on behalf of the caller —
  used by integration tests / scripted attestations).

The signed bytes are the canonical JSON of the claim payload built
from ``kind`` / ``description`` / ``tradition`` / ``grade_or_degree``
/ ``granted_at`` / ``subject_*``. Server and client both compute
those bytes from the same recipe (:func:`canonical_attestation_bytes`).

private_key.description: 32 raw Ed25519 private key bytes. Server-side signing path. Server discards the key after use; never persisted.
private_key.title: Private Key

self_signature.description: 64 raw Ed25519 signature bytes (over canonical claim).
self_signature.title: Self Signature

AttestationCreate.title: AttestationCreate

days_with_banishing_ratio.description: days_with_banishing / window_days; None when window is zero.
days_with_banishing_ratio.title: Days With Banishing Ratio

selected_refs.description: JSON array of item refs to import. Omit to import everything.
selected_refs.title: Selected Refs

BundleUninstallResponse.description:
Response of ``DELETE /bundles/installed/{id}`` — explicit about
what was and was not removed (tombstone, not erasure).
BundleUninstallResponse.title: BundleUninstallResponse

BundledPackageRead.description: One of the content packages that ship with Theourgia.
BundledPackageRead.title: BundledPackageRead

CalendarDateRead.description: A single calendar's view of a single instant.
CalendarDateRead.title: CalendarDateRead

accuracy_rate.description: correct / (correct + incorrect). None when the denominator is zero. Ambiguous outcomes are not counted in either bucket.
accuracy_rate.title: Accuracy Rate

CardUpdate.description: PATCH one card. All fields optional. b108-2hc.
CardUpdate.title: CardUpdate

CipherLanguage.description: The seven cipher language families recognised by Phase 08.
CipherLanguage.title: CipherLanguage

CipherUpdate.description:
Bundled ciphers cannot be updated — PATCH returns 409.

For per-vault rows, every field except ``personal`` and
``bundled_slug`` is patchable. The ``personal`` flag is recomputed
from the patched ``source_citation`` on save.
CipherUpdate.title: CipherUpdate

CommentPublicRead.description: Public projection — safe for the anonymous reader.
CommentPublicRead.title: CommentPublicRead

CompassTradition.description: The five compass-point traditions the H05 spec accepts.
CompassTradition.title: CompassTradition

civil_date.description: Local civil date the completion belongs to. Defaults to today in the supplied timezone. Pass an explicit value for backfill.
civil_date.title: Civil Date

ConfigureBody.description:
A bag of key/value settings to merge into the plugin's
``plugin_setting`` rows. Existing keys are overwritten; absent
keys are untouched.
ConfigureBody.title: ConfigureBody

CurrentKeyRead.description: The vault's active DEK — identifiers only, never material.
CurrentKeyRead.title: CurrentKeyRead

DayOneImportPayload.description: Raw Day One JSON export handed to the importer.
DayOneImportPayload.title: DayOneImportPayload

DigestItemUpdate.description: Only ``dismissed`` is editable on a digest item.
DigestItemUpdate.title: DigestItemUpdate

DownloadResult.description:
B127 ships this as a JSON contract; the real PDF stream
arrives in a follow-up batch that adds the asset pipeline.
For now the response carries the asset URL the client follows
+ the per-purchase download_count after this fetch.
DownloadResult.title: DownloadResult

EntityAggregate.description:
Read-side projection: focus entity + alias-graph neighbours +
any saved views that include the focus entity.

Per ``plan/05-magical-beings.md`` §11. Workings, offerings,
contracts always attach to one specific entity_id at write time;
this endpoint resolves the multi-entity view at read time.

member_entity_ids.description: All entity_ids that should be considered together when aggregating offerings / contracts / workings. Always contains the focus id, plus any same-as / aspect-of / aspect-includes / syncretic-with neighbours, plus the members of every saved EntityView that contains the focus.
member_entity_ids.title: Member Entity Ids

views.description: EntityView ids that include the focus entity.
views.title: Views

EntityAggregate.title: EntityAggregate

EntryBodyUpdate.description: PATCH body only — used by the Tiptap auto-save loop.
EntryBodyUpdate.title: EntryBodyUpdate

EntryCreate.description:
Body for ``POST /api/v1/entries`` — mirrors frontend ``CreateEntryInput``.

Phase 04 extras are optional; pre-Phase-04 client payloads still
validate because every new field has a default.
EntryCreate.title: EntryCreate

EntryRead.description:
Wire format for a single entry — mirrors frontend ``EntryRecord``.

Phase 04 expands this with optional fields. Pre-Phase-04 clients
that don't ask for the new fields keep working because every new
column is `Optional` and `default=None`.
EntryRead.title: EntryRead

EntrySealRequest.description:
Body of ``POST /entries/{id}/seal`` — the client-encrypted
envelope string. Never plaintext; the server stores it opaquely.

The 4 MB cap mirrors the 2 MB plaintext body cap with headroom
for base64 expansion + the envelope wrapper.
EntrySealRequest.title: EntrySealRequest

EntrySealedPayloadRead.description:
Ciphertext-only read model — there is no plaintext field to
leak by construction.
EntrySealedPayloadRead.title: EntrySealedPayloadRead

EntryWindowCounts.description: Counts in a time window — total + per-type breakdown.
EntryWindowCounts.title: EntryWindowCounts

FamilyTree.description: Kinship graph reachable from a probe entity, bounded by generation.
FamilyTree.title: FamilyTree

FamilyTreeNode.description:
One entity node in the tree with its generation offset from the probe.

Generation 0 is the probe, -1 is one generation up (parent),
+1 is one generation down (child). Siblings + spouses share
generation with their kinship counterpart.
FamilyTreeNode.title: FamilyTreeNode

fed_at.description: Defaults to server time at receipt.
fed_at.title: Fed At

FigureRef.description: Slim figure reference embedded in a reading.

HubMembershipPolicy.description:
How members join a hub (H08 surface 3 + 5).

``PUBLIC`` — anyone can join immediately, no approval step.

``PRIVATE`` — invite-only. **The default.**

The capability-review surface POSTs this after the magician has

The card-shape returned in the list endpoint.

For sealed assets, the H07 rule is COUNT-ONLY in the list. We

Single-asset read response.

For sealed assets we omit alt_text / caption / exif_metadata /
dimensions / tags — the body is encrypted client-side. We still
return the R2 object key (the client decrypts after fetching),
the kind, the size, and the ``sealed: true`` indicator.

Direct metadata create. The R2 upload pipeline (B133) will
create the row through a higher-level flow; this endpoint is the
primitive.

The list endpoint always carries the sealed_count separately.

Sealed assets are NEVER enumerated as cards; the count is the
only visible signal.

MediaUpdate.description:
PATCH fields. r2_object_key / size_bytes / mime_type / kind
/ owner_id are intentionally absent — those are immutable per
the plan.

asked_at.description: The chart instant. Defaults to server time at receipt.
asked_at.title: Asked At

seed.description: Explicit seed. If omitted, derived from SHA-256(timestamp || question || uuid). Same seed + same method = same six lines.
seed.title: Seed

seed.description: Explicit seed. If omitted, derived from the request: for hash_of_question, SHA-256 of (timestamp + question); for browser_rng, a random UUID is used.
seed.title: Seed

theourgia__api__routers__v1__user_sessio.description: Per-device shape. RULE 48 — NO token IDs ever; UUIDs minimum.
theourgia__api__routers__v1__user_sessio.title: SessionRead

info.description:
Theourgia — a magickal journal CMS and full practitioner toolkit.

This API exposes the journal, entities, divinations, sigils, federation
primitives, and admin surfaces of a Theourgia instance. Versioned under
``/api/v1``. Authentication uses bearer tokens issued by the session
endpoints.

Open source, federated, self-hostable. AGPL-3.0 forever.
info.title: Theourgia

get.description: Returns this instance's federation public key and DID. Used by federation peers to verify signed exchanges. Unauthenticated; cacheable for one hour.

schema.title: Response List Follow Requests Api V1 Activitypub Follow Requests Get
Response List Followers Api V1 Activitypub Followers Get

get.description: Audit log for the calling user's vault — proxies to daemon GET /audit.

Aggregated cost + token rollup for the C10 dashboard — proxies
to daemon GET /costs/summary scoped to the caller's vault.

post.description:
Start an agent run — proxies to daemon POST /runs.

The vault adds the DID resolution + vault session token; the daemon
holds the cap state, MCP session, subprocess.

get.description: SSE relay — forwards the daemon's event-stream verbatim.

schema.title: Response List Altars Api V1 Altars Get

post.description:
Pearson + Spearman matrix over a fixed set of numeric axes
on the synchronicity subject. v1 ships only intensity +
weekday_num; future revisions extend the axis catalog as new
numeric columns are materialised.

Run a query against the caller's vault and return the result
without persisting a snapshot. Use ``POST /studies/{id}/run`` to
persist.

get.description:
Counts for the calendar day. The H06 Analytics Dashboard's
hero strip wants these three numbers; the route caps the
sample window at the current calendar day in UTC.
Compute a natal/event chart for the supplied instant + location.

post.description:
Find the most magickally favorable instants in the time window
that satisfy the user-supplied constraints, or pre-built recipe.

get.description: Current sky state. Convenience wrapper around /astro/chart.
24 planetary hours for the date containing ``when`` (or now).

schema.title: Response List Attestations Api V1 Attestations Get

post.description:
Create an attestation and persist its self-signature.

The signer's public key must be present; the signature can either
be supplied directly or computed server-side from the private key
(the latter is the test path).

get.description:
Verify every signature on this attestation.

Returns per-signature status so the UI can show "L. Vespera's
signature: ✓ valid" / "Original initiator key: ✗ does not verify".

Attachment detail — includes ``transcript`` +
``transcript_engine`` so the editor can render provenance-tagged
transcripts (and poll for a queued one).

post.description:
Queue local Whisper transcription for one attachment.

202 on enqueue. 403 when the instance gate or the user opt-in
gate is closed (distinct details). 409 when a transcript already
exists and ``force`` is not set.
Find-or-create a development user with the supplied magickal name and open a session. PHASE 02 ONLY — replaced by the WebAuthn ceremony in a later batch.

get.summary: Whether a password is set for the current account

put.description: Sets an Argon2id password hash. If a password is already set, ``current_password`` must verify against the stored hash. New passwords must be at least 8 characters.
put.summary: Set or change the account password

delete.description: Revoke the cookie-presented session and clear the cookie.

get.description: Return the SessionRead for the cookie-presented session, or 401.

delete.description: Disable TOTP + delete all backup codes.

post.description: Regenerate the 10 backup codes. Revokes any previous set.

Issue a fresh TOTP secret + provisioning URI.

The secret is persisted immediately on the user row as a
*provisional* value. It becomes usable only after `/verify`.
Re-calling `/begin` before `/verify` rotates the provisional
secret (previous QR becomes invalid).

Verify a TOTP code OR a backup code at sign-in.

Assumes primary auth (WebAuthn / password / demo signin) already
established a session. This endpoint is the second-factor gate.

Returns `used_backup_code=True` if the caller consumed one of the
10-per-user backup codes; that row is marked `used_at=now()` and
cannot be reused.

Confirm enrolment by verifying a code + issue backup codes.

On success, the previously-provisional TOTP secret is now the
canonical second-factor secret. 10 backup codes are minted and
returned once; only their SHA-256 hashes persist.

Anonymous — issue an assertion challenge.

Discoverable flow: no allow_credentials list, the authenticator
picks the credential via the passkey UI. The browser returns the
user handle we set at registration time so the finish endpoint
knows which user to sign in.

schema.title: Response Assert Begin Api V1 Auth Webauthn Assert Begin Post
Response Register Begin Api V1 Auth Webauthn Register Begin Post
Response List Readings Api V1 Bibliomancy Readings Get

get.description: JSON Feed 1.1 representation.

schema.title: Response Json Feed Api V1 Blog Feed Json Get

get.description: RSS 2.0 feed of the most recent 50 public blog posts.
Atom 1.0 feed of the most recent 50 public blog posts.

One public post + its full body.

Same filters as the list endpoint (visibility=public,
non-encrypted, not deleted, scheduled window elapsed).
Non-matching IDs 404 rather than 403 so we don't disclose
the existence of a private entry.

schema.title: Response List Books Api V1 Books Get

get.description:
The seven content packages that ship with Theourgia — real
``.mbf`` containers built at runtime from cited public-domain
sources (see :mod:`theourgia.core.bundles.bundled_content` for
the diligence record).

post.description:
Import one bundled content package wholesale.

Runs the standard import path: entities land as immutable nodes
with ``origin="imported_from_bundle:<slug>@<version>"``, imported
content is always personal-visibility, and payload kinds without
a v1 importer (``correspondences``, ``dream-symbols``) are
listed-not-imported with an honest per-item report — never
silently dropped.

get.description:
Build an ``.mbf`` from vault content of one type.

``sign=true`` signs with the instance Ed25519 federation keypair
(opt-in). Closed-tradition declarations survive export: items
whose tags intersect this instance's closed list stamp the
manifest ``closed_tradition: true``.

parameters.description: Sign with the instance federation keypair
parameters.name: sign

schema.description: Sign with the instance federation keypair
schema.title: Sign

post.description:
Commit a user-selected subset of items (piecemeal by design).

Imported content is always personal-visibility. Entities become
immutable nodes with ``origin="imported_from_bundle:<slug>@
<version>"``; alias prompting defaults to ``distinct`` (v1
creates no alias rows). The install record persists attribution
and the verbatim provenance chain.

get.description: The vault's install records — attribution always present.

delete.description:
Remove the install record — and nothing else.

Imported content (entities, templates, recipes, …) persists in
the vault per the MBF tombstone-not-erasure rule: imported
entities are immutable provenance-stamped nodes the user may
have already linked from entries, and uninstalling a bundle must
never silently delete vault content. Owner-only; other users'
install records 404.

post.description:
Upload, validate, verify — no writes.

Returns the manifest, signature verdict (unsigned is a verdict:
warn, don't block), per-payload item listings, the license +
attribution block, the closed-tradition declaration with the
respect-source notice, and conflicts (existing same-name entities
per the alias-graph model; existing same-slug installed bundles).

get.description: Today (or any supplied instant) in every registered calendar.
List ciphers visible to the caller — bundled + personal.

schema.title: Response List Ciphers Api V1 Ciphers Get

post.description:
Create a per-vault cipher.

Honesty rule: ``personal`` is derived from ``source_citation`` —
empty/null → ``personal=true``; non-empty → ``personal=false``.

get.description: Return the bundled PD corpus — reference material, no auth.

schema.title: Response List Bundled Ciphers Api V1 Ciphers Bundled Get
Response List Circles Api V1 Circles Get

get.description:
Return the preset library — PD templates the practitioner can
fork from. No auth scoping; presets are reference material.

get.summary: List APPROVED comments for a target

schema.title: Response List Contracts Api V1 Contracts Get

post.description:
Flip a single obligation to ``fulfilled`` (or any
:class:`ObligationStatus`).

The endpoint is named ``fulfill-obligation`` because that's the
overwhelmingly common case; supplying ``new_status`` lets the same
endpoint mark obligations as ``in-progress`` / ``overdue`` / ``waived``.

get.description:
CSV export of the user's own audit log. Forensic artefact —

max.

post.description:
Run the GDPR export inline + return the archive JSON.

Rule 45 says async-with-email; the v1 endpoint returns inline so
the H10 DataExportRequest surface has a stable contract. The
surface dispatches the JSON to the user as a downloadable blob.

get.description:
The caller's default persona — the editor's fallback author
when no persona is explicitly picked.

post.description: Revoke every active session except the one making this request.

get.description:
List the caller's media. Sealed assets surface as a count
only (per the H07 Media Library rule).

Standalone sealed-asset count. The H07 Media Library card
pulls this on its own without paging the list.

delete.description:
Cancel an in-flight upload. Idempotent on already-CANCELLED.
Refuses to cancel an already-COMPLETED upload (409) — use
DELETE /media/{id} to soft-delete a completed asset.

Soft delete. The R2 lifecycle policy (B133) reclaims bytes
after 30 days; until then the row is restorable.

post.description:
Record a check-in. Bumps ``last_check_in_at`` to now.

Even a memorialized vault accepts check-ins; if the operator
signs in from beyond the veil, they may want to reactivate.

v1-018: also clears ``warning_notified_at`` — a check-in starts a
fresh cadence cycle, so a future lapse gets its reminder again.

Split a client-supplied secret into executor shares.

The shares are returned ONCE and never stored; hand them to the
executor(s) out-of-band. Only a SHA-256 commitment + parameters
persist. Calling again replaces the commitment — previously
distributed shares stop verifying.

Check a client-side reconstruction against the commitment.

Stores nothing; the candidate secret exists only for the duration
of the request. Combining fewer than k shares yields a value that
fails here — the commitment is the only integrity signal (Shamir
reconstruction itself cannot detect wrong shares).

Bring a memorialized vault back. Bumps last_check_in_at too so
the state immediately returns to `active`.

v1-018: clears both notification markers so a future lapse cycle
dispatches the reminder and the executor notice afresh.

Enter memorial mode manually. Destructive: private writes are
frozen; the vault becomes a read-only in-memoriam surface until
a subsequent /reactivate call.

get.description: Version, capabilities, and identity of this Theourgia instance.

Cancel a SCHEDULED issue. CANCELLED is terminal; cloning is

Render the issue to a single recipient. NEVER touches the
issue's status, recipient_count, or sent_at.

Flip DRAFT → SENDING + dispatch the delivery task.

The H07 surface contract: the response always carries
``confirmation_required=true`` so the surface displays the
--warn-soft confirm modal before the practitioner reaches
this endpoint. The route itself is matter-of-fact; the
confirmation is enforced surface-side.

Response List Routes Api V1 Pilgrimage Routes Get

post.summary: Reorder stops via full ordered id list

get.description:
Map data list. Sealed sites are STRIPPED here — they appear

post.description:
Count-only endpoint. Returns ONLY the number of sealed sites
in the requested map bounds (no ids, no coords). The H07
Pilgrimage Map's sealed-badge data source.

patch.description:
PATCH does NOT touch location or precision — only name /
story / linked_working_ids. The schema doesn't even expose the
location fields.

post.description:
Lower the site's precision floor.

REJECTS any transition that would make precision FINER. The
previous lat/lng are overwritten with the new quantized values
— the finer precision is irreversibly lost (the whole point).

Flip ``sealed=True``. The site stops appearing on map data
immediately. Unseal happens via the Mode B vault crypto path
(B108) on the client; the server endpoint trusts the client
state.

Fetch a release from the registry, VERIFY it, unpack, record.

Verification chain (all failures → 400, nothing touches disk):

  1. sha256 of the downloaded bytes matches the registry's header
     (and is recomputed locally — the header is cross-checked, not
     trusted).
  2. The author's Ed25519 signature over the domain-separated
     artifact payload verifies against the public key pinned in the
     registry record.
  3. The archive unpacks safely (no traversal/links/bombs) and its
     ``plugin.toml`` parses STRICTLY, with name + version matching
     the requested release.

Policy: install-time signature failure is **warn-not-block for
BUNDLES** (data imports — see the MBF importer) but **block for
plugin CODE** (plan/14 § 5 signed releases). This endpoint installs
code, so unsigned or badly-signed releases are refused outright.

``version`` omitted → the newest accepted release with an uploaded
artifact. Registry refusals pass through with their status (410
tombstone/withdrawn keeps the author's reason).

get.description:
Proxy the registry's public browse into the search contract.

``version`` maps from the registry card's ``latest_version`` (the
newest accepted release); plugins with no accepted release yet show
an em dash. Unconfigured registry → 503, unreachable → 502 (same
honesty mapping as the registry bridge).

schema.title: Response List Assertions Api V1 Sso Assertions Get

post.description: Issue a new assertion. Server fixes expires_at_utc = now + 24h.

Mint a vault-signed SSO assertion for the plugin registry.

The registry bridge half of v1-032: the client POSTs the returned
``{assertion, signature_b64}`` pair to the registry's
``/api/v1/auth/sso-session`` (``registry_sso_url``) and receives an
author session mapped to this vault's author DID.

The assertion is signed with the instance's FEDERATION keypair —
the key the registry verifies against this host's
``/.well-known/theourgia/actor`` document. It carries the author's
registry signing public key (when configured) so first-contact SSO
can bootstrap the author's key at the registry; the registry never
overwrites an existing key via this path.

503 when the registry URL or the author identity isn't configured —
same honesty copy as the A-cluster routes.

that ``/gematria/search`` produces; for ``gematria_calculation``
it records the per-cipher breakdown. Execution is performed
HERE (server-side) so the snapshot reflects the state of the
practitioner's vault at run-time.

schema.title: Response List Talismans Api V1 Talismans Get

post.description:
Fork a new version with ``parent_talisman_id`` set.

Forking a sealed talisman initialises the child sealed with the
same ciphertext + IV — the practitioner must re-encrypt if they
want the fork to diverge. Forking a plaintext talisman copies
the composition.

The consecration link is **not** inherited (forks default to
not-consecrated; the practitioner must re-link if applicable).

Switch a talisman into sealed (Mode B) mode.

The client has already encrypted ``{front_svg, back_svg,
components}`` with the vault key and is POSTing the ciphertext +
IV. We:
  1. store the ciphertext + IV
  2. set encryption_mode = SEALED
  3. NULL out the plaintext columns (the server must never see
     the cleartext again)

Return the ciphertext + IV so the client can decrypt in
memory. The row stays sealed — this is read-only.

get.description:
List built-in decks + the caller's own decks.

put.summary: Update the signed-in user's enabled calendars

get.description: Returns the user's stored astrological location, or Greenwich when unset.

post.description:
Public-facing double-opt-in entry. Anonymous.

If a subscriber already exists for this (owner_id, email):
  - PENDING_CONFIRMATION → reuse the row (don't issue a fresh
    token; the existing one is still valid).
  - ACTIVE → no-op acknowledgment (don't reveal that the
    email is already on the list).
  - UNSUBSCRIBED → re-subscribe with FRESH tokens + flip
    status back to PENDING_CONFIRMATION (per the plan, re-
    subscribing requires a new signup with new tokens).
  - FAILED_PAYMENT → respond as if pending (the publisher's
    side already has it surfaced; the subscriber doesn't need
    to know about the failure).

schema.title: Response List Voces Api V1 Voces Get

get.description: Return the bundled PD corpus — reference material, no auth.

schema.title: Response List Bundled Voces Api V1 Voces Bundled Get

post.description:
Fork a bundled fixture into the practitioner's vault.

The new row preserves the bundled fixture's text, transliteration,
IPA, and citation — and records the bundled id in
``forked_from_bundled_id`` for provenance.

get.description:
Return the calling owner's per-vault state for this voce.

If no row exists yet, the default ``{ private_note: null,
hidden: false }`` is returned. Unauthenticated callers get a
401.

put.description:
Upsert the calling owner's per-vault state.

Idempotent: PUT twice → one row, not two (uniqueness enforced
by ``uq_voce_per_vault``). Absent fields in the payload do NOT
overwrite existing values.

get.description: Fetches a normalised open-meteo snapshot for the given coordinates. Returns 200 with ``snapshot: null`` when the upstream fetch fails or the coordinates are out of coverage — never surfaces a 5xx to the frontend.

Returns whether the opt-in nudge is enabled, whether it should show right now, and the support-resource starter list. Opted-out users get enabled=false, show=false, resources=[] — and their mood data is never queried.

put.description: Persists the a11y.crisis_nudge user setting. Enabling also clears any mute horizon.

post.description: Sets a11y.crisis_nudge_muted_until to an ISO date or 'forever' (the default). While muted, GET returns show=false and no trigger evaluation happens.

get.description:
Deliver the VCALENDAR file. Private feeds require the caller's
auth cookie (401 otherwise). Public feeds skip auth.

Live data walking is NOT in scope for B135 — that's an integration
surface in B136. For now the feed always emits a minimal
VCALENDAR shell so subscribers' clients don't error out.

Returns the instance's Prometheus metrics in the text exposition format. Requires admin scope. **Off by default for public consumption** to avoid fingerprinting; operators who run a metrics sidecar set up their own scrape with an admin token.

Return the AP actor document.

Content negotiation: if Accept lacks `activity+json`, redirect to
the human profile page. This keeps the URL universal — same id
works for AP servers and browsers.
Return a stub collection. Rule 9: no public follower counts.

Always returns an empty collection.

Theourgia does not federate-FOLLOW other AP actors (rule: consent-
first follows are inbound only). The endpoint exists so AP clients
that probe it don't 404 the entire actor.
Some AP probes do a GET first. Return a minimal OrderedCollection.

post.description:
Accept an AP-format activity addressed to this actor.

v1-026: the request MUST carry a valid HTTP signature (the same
RFC 9421 subset the native inbox verifies) unless the operator has
flipped the ``federation.accept_anonymous_inbound`` instance
setting. Unsigned / invalid → 401. Verified requests record a
replay nonce and persist to federation_activity where the inbox
processor drains them out-of-band; unclassifiable types stash as
kind=UNKNOWN so the operator always has a record.

get.description:
Paged OrderedCollection of public blog posts.

Rule 12: AP only sees Visibility=PUBLIC.
Rule 38: strictly chronological order (no popularity rank).
